title: deadline budget steps tokens wall clock
purpose: A1.4 defines deadline budget steps tokens wall clock for the agent-driven expert system. Pursuit & control plane — autonomous loops until goal verified.
scope: Owns `A1.4` only; siblings under `A1` must not duplicate this spec.
Aligns with minimal HITL: H1 plan, H2 blocker, H3 sign-off ([INTRO-1.2](INTRO-1.2-human-touchpoint-contract-h1-h2-h3.md)).
Conflicts resolve in favor of [Vision §3 — Branch A — Pursuit & control plane](../../full-automation-vision-and-hierarchy.md#3-branch-a-pursuit-control-plane).
behavior_steps: At H1 plan approval, the operator sets deadline and budget fields—max_steps, max_tokens, and wall-clock deadline—in state.goal alongside [A1.2](A1.2-success-criteria-machine-checkable.md) success criteria so [A3.2](A3.2-goal-autopilot-until-goal-verify-or-hard-block.md) pursuit is bounded instead of unbounded.

On each wake, [A2.1](A2.1-preflight-check-pipeline-blocked-extended.md) compares live counters against those caps before routing; an exhausted budget or passed deadline fires an [A4.3](A4.3-stop-resource-max-steps-max-cost-lease-expired.md) resource stop instead of scheduling another pipeline phase.

After [A2.3](A2.3-post-step-route-tier-dual-write-increment.md) dual-write, pursuit increments step count, token usage, and elapsed wall-clock time in state.json so restarts resume accounting from the last good snapshot.

Plane D scheduling reads the same budget fields so self-improvement platform turns stay bounded and do not displace the active project goal during [A3.2](A3.2-goal-autopilot-until-goal-verify-or-hard-block.md) goal autopilot.

When caps are exhausted and human intervention can extend them, pursuit surfaces H2 with the structured stop reason; if budget fields are missing, contradictory, or counters drift from journal evidence, preflight fails closed at H2 rather than continuing unbounded pursuit.

goal_verify passes but H3 rejected â€” goal.state returns to pursuing with rejection notes.
Edge case `A1.4` variant 4: verify state dual-write before continuing pursuit.
Pass 3: add regression test or evidence path specific to `A1.4`.
Pass 3: cross-link related nodes in same branch index.

**False complete:** Item marked done without artifact â†’ audit-hierarchy-depth.py re-enqueues deepen pass.
**Scope bleed:** Worker edits journal/state during planning-only expansion â†’ forbidden in vision-expansion-prompt.
**Stale design:** Upstream vision Â§ changes â†’ reconcile-stale adds deepen items for affected ids.
implementation: Add `goal` block to state template and journal-keeper dual-write (v2.14).
Implement `scripts/goal-verify.py` stub aggregating task evidence paths.
Document `A1.4` in parent index with verify command and release tag.
Add checklist row in SEC-15 release doc for `A1.4`.
acceptance: [ ] Named script, skill, or test path exists or is listed in SEC-15 release row
narrative: A1.4 defines deadline budget steps tokens wall clock for the agent-driven expert system. Pursuit & control plane — autonomous loops until goal verified. In practice, the system at h1 plan approval, the operator sets deadline and budget fields—max_steps, max_tokens, and wall-clock deadline—in state.goal alongside [a1.2](a1.2-success-criteria-machine-checkable.md) success criteria so [a3.2](a3.2-goal-autopilot-until-goal-verify-or-hard-block.md) pursuit is bounded instead of unbounded, then on each wake, [a2.1](a2.1-preflight-check-pipeline-blocked-extended.md) compares live counters against those caps before routing; an exhausted budget or passed deadline fires an [a4.3](a4.3-stop-resource-max-steps-max-cost-lease-expired.md) resource stop instead of scheduling another pipeline phase.
reader_narrative:
<!-- prose-source: agent pilot 2026-06-28 -->

Autonomous pursuit without bounds can consume unbounded tokens, steps, and wall-clock time. This capability defines deadline and budget fields—steps, tokens, wall clock—so the loop can stop cleanly with a recorded reason instead of running indefinitely.

Budgets are enforced at preflight and after each turn. When a budget is exhausted, pursuit stops, dual-writes a structured stop reason, and surfaces H2 only if human intervention can extend the budget. Deadlines align with operator expectations for time-sensitive milestones.

These fields turn “always-on” into “always-on until provably done or bounded.” They also inform Plane D scheduling so self-improvement work stays bounded and does not displace the current project goal. Incorrect budget configuration is an operational edge case: too tight causes false stops; too loose wastes resources.
